message:
feat(runtime): add weak RuntimeHandle variant to prevent reference cycles

Introduce RuntimeHandleRef enum with Strong(Arc) and Weak(Weak)
variants, allowing RuntimeHandle to hold either a strong or weak
reference to RuntimeInner. This prevents reference cycles when the
runtime handle is stored inside structures that the runtime itself owns
(e.g. spawned tasks that capture the handle).

Key changes:
- RuntimeHandle now wraps RuntimeHandleRef instead of a bare Arc
- Add RuntimeHandle::strong() and RuntimeHandle::weak() constructors
- RuntimeHandle::spawn() upgrades weak refs on demand, returning an
  error if the runtime has been dropped
- Worker threads receive strong handles; the thread-local
  ScopedRuntimeHandle stores the strong variant
- RuntimeInner::new() is now infallible (returns Self instead of
  io::Result<Self>) since all fallible initialization has been moved
  to spawn_worker_threads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
